items.title: Benfica verkoopt uitzendrechten en sluit recorddeal in Portugal
items.summary: Benfica heeft een historisch akkoord bereikt met het Portugese NOS over de verkoop van de televisierechten voor de seizoenen 2026/27 en 2027/28. De tv-deal is met een totale waarde van 114,2 miljoen euro de grootste ooit in Portugal.

items.title: UEFA weigert Europa League-duel PAOK uit te stellen na dodelijk ongeval
items.summary: De UEFA is niet van plan het Europa League-duel van PAOK Saloniki met Olympique Lyon te verplaatsen. De Griekse club verzocht de Europese voetbalbond de wedstrijd uit te stellen, nadat zeven supporters onderweg naar Frankrijk omkwamen bij een verkeersongeluk.

items.title: KV Mechelen presenteert Boersma, Vitesse laat verdediger vertrekken
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Blik op de coëfficiënten: Portugal is klaar voor de genadeklap
items.summary: De coëfficiëntenpolonaise verandert langzaam in een rouwstoet. Na de resultaten van vorige week staat de zesde plaats van Nederland meer dan ooit onder druk. Met de laatste speelronde in de competitiefase van de Champions League en Europa League voor de boeg schetsen we de stand van zaken in de strijd met Portugal.

items.title: 'Middenveld PSV puzzelwerk voor Peter Bosz'
items.summary: Peter Bosz gaf eerder aan dat hij het liefst met elf middenvelders speelt, maar welke drie namen vervullen tegen Bayern München het centrum? Keert Ismael Saibari terug op tien? Behoudt Paul Wanner zijn plek? En is Salah-Eddine fit genoeg dat Mauro Junior weer terug het middenveld op kan? En hoe zit het met de spitspositie? Genoeg vragen om over te speculeren in de nieuwe VI ZSM met Rob Aarts en Inta Janssen.

items.title: Chelsea urge fans to take ‘extreme caution’ in Naples after two taken to hospital
items.summary: Treatment needed for ‘non-life-threatening injuries’ Chelsea fans report having knives pulled on them Chelsea have told fans to take “extreme caution” in Naples after two supporters were treated in hospital there before their Champions League tie in the Italian city. Napoli and Chelsea meet at the Diego Armando Maradona Stadium on Wednesday night in the competition’s final round of group games. Continue reading...

items.title: Selling stars - how Man City have turned player sales into big business
items.summary: Manchester City have made more than £250m selling young players from their talent factory over the last five years.

items.title: A strict and successful disciplinarian – Liverpool face the ‘Azeri Sir Alex Ferguson’
items.summary: Gurban Gurbanov has been in charge of Qarabag since 2008 and continues to prioritise a squad mentality ahead of star signings Remember the summer of 2008? That was when Pep Guardiola was appointed as first-team coach at Barcelona, Jürgen Klopp arrived at Borussia Dortmund and Chelsea gambled on a certain Luiz Felipe Scolari. Since then the west London club have had 12 different permanent managers. Qarabag, who Liverpool play in the Champions League on Wednesday, have had one: Gurban Gurbanov, also known as the Azeri Sir Alex Ferguson. Gurbanov has become one of the longest serving elite coaches in the world and he has built a remarkable empire, turning the club into a force to be reckoned with in European competitions. Continue reading...

items.title: Which football league had the fewest teams finishing with a positive GD? | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: two sets of fathers and sons involved in one match, more record wins and losses and ‘sixes and sevens’ Mail us with your questions and answers “Twelve of the 18 Bundesliga teams have a negative goal difference,” notes Damian Cerase. “I suppose this is down to Bayern handing out weekly drubbings, given that their GD is +57 after only 18 games. What’s the greatest disparity in a full season between the number of teams registering positive or negative GDs?” “At the time of writing in the Bundesliga, all teams haven’t quite played the same number of games but nevertheless 66.6% of the teams have a negative goal difference,” begins Chris Roe. “For a complete season, the highest percentage in the English league system is from tier two in 2005-06 when 17 of the 24 teams (70.83%) had a negative goal difference; no doubt this was in part due to champions Reading, who had a +67 goal difference for the season. This example is narrowly ahead of two Premier League seasons (1998-99 and 2017-18) when six of the 14 (or 70%) had negative GD at the end of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: Australian Open LIVE! Sinner clinches first set against Shelton in last quarter-final
O'Connor signs contract extension with Leeds
items.summary: Leeds Rhinos hooker Jarrod O'Connor has agreed a new two-year contract extension that will keep him with the club until at least the end of the 2028 Betfred Super League season.

items.title: Rodman, Hayes viral celebration caps off U.S. rout
items.summary: Trinity Rodman capped off the USWNT's 5-0 rout of Chile by coaxing coach Emma Hayes into a sideline dance, as a young American squad secured its fifth consecutive shutout Tuesday night.

items.title: Abraham joins Villa 1 day after Besiktas transfer
items.summary: Tammy Abraham has signed for Aston Villa from Besiktas just one day after he joined the Turkish club a permanent transfer from Roma.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal had 'beautiful' meeting after loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed he called his players for a meeting to "bring down the temperature" in the wake of their defeat to Manchester United.

items.summary: There is less than a week to go in the January transfer window. Here are the Premier League players who still need a move away this month.

items.summary: Premier League clubs are corporations that have outgrown the "manager" role that legendary clubs once had. Why?

items.title: Two fans in hospital as Chelsea urge caution after Naples 'incident'
items.summary: Travelling Chelsea fans in Naples for Wednesday's Champions League game are told to "exercise extreme caution" after two supporters suffer injuries.

items.title: Tottenham’s Champions League preparations rocked by injuries and car crash
items.summary: Porro and Van de Ven join injury list for Frankfurt game Kolo Muani and Odobert unharmed after tyre blowout Thomas Frank’s preparations for the Champions League game at Eintracht Frankfurt on Wednesday night have been rocked by injuries to Pedro Porro and Micky van de Ven – and a car crash affecting Randal Kolo Muani and Wilson Odobert. The Tottenham manager reported that Kolo Muani and Odobert were OK after pictures emerged on social media of them standing by a black Ferrari with significant damage to its front end. Continue reading...
